mnemonics: Fork A Zippy Elephant (Fork, Exec, Zombie, Exit)
Parent's Patience Pays Off (Use wait() to prevent zombies)
keyPoints: A process is created using the fork() system call, which creates a child process as a duplicate of the parent.
The exec() family of functions replaces the current process image with a new one, loading and running a different program.
A parent process should use wait() or waitpid() to collect the exit status of its child processes, preventing zombie processes.
commonMistakes: Not checking the return value of fork(), leading to confusion between parent and child execution paths.
Forgetting that exec() replaces the current process; any code after a successful exec call will not be executed.
Creating zombie processes by forking without subsequently calling wait() in the parent to reap the terminated child.
